revision.text: '''大鳥居王子'''（おおとりいおうじ）は[[九十九王子]]の8番目で現存していない。跡地は[[堺市]]鳳東町7のＮＴＴ鳳営業所といわれているが確証はない。名前の由来はすぐ近くの[[大鳥大社]]からと思われる。現在は大鳥大社境内の式内社・大鳥美波比神社に合祀されている。

'''大鳥居王子'''（おおとりいおうじ）は[[九十九王子]]の8番目で現存していない。跡地は[[堺市]]鳳東町7のＮＴＴ鳳営業所といわれているが確証はない。名前の由来はすぐ近くの[[大鳥大社]]からと思われる。現在は大鳥大社境内の式内社・大鳥美波比神社に合祀されている。
*最寄駅 [[JR]][[阪和線]][[鳳駅]]

'''大鳥居王子'''（'''おおとりいおうじ'''、'''大鳥王子'''とも）は、[[九十九王子]]の8番目で現存していない。跡地は[[大阪府]][[堺市]]鳳東町7のNTT鳳営業所といわれているが確証はない。名前の由来はすぐ近くの[[大鳥大社]]からと思われる。現在は大鳥大社境内の式内社・大鳥美波比神社に合祀されている。
*最寄駅 [[JR]][[阪和線]][[鳳駅]]

'''大鳥居王子'''（'''おおとりいおうじ'''、'''大鳥王子'''とも）は、[[九十九王子]]の8番目で現存していない。跡地は[[大阪府]][[堺市]]鳳東町7のNTT鳳営業所といわれているが確証はない。名前の由来はすぐ近くの[[大鳥大社]]からと思われる。現在は大鳥大社境内の式内社・大鳥美波比神社に合祀されている。

'''大鳥居王子'''（'''おおとりいおうじ'''、'''大鳥王子'''とも）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。街道の起点にある[[窪津王子]]から数えて8番目。
その存在は、[[1201年]]（建仁元年）、後鳥羽上皇の熊野御幸の記録『後鳥羽院熊野御幸記』に「大鳥居新王子」として記されており、この頃に比較的近い時期に設けられたものと推定される。「大鳥居」の名称は、付近の[[大鳥神社]]とその鳥居に由来するとの説が有力である。
その位置は、江戸時代まで存在した北王子村にあった大鳥美波比神社（現在は大鳥神社に合祀）といわれている。現在の[[堺市]]鳳東町7のNTT鳳営業所付近と考えられる。
*最寄駅 [[JR]][[阪和線]][[鳳駅]]
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:大阪府の神社|おおとりいおうし]] [[Category:堺市|おおとりいおうし]] [[Category:九十九王子|おおとりいおうし]]

'''大鳥居王子'''（'''おおとりいおうじ'''、'''大鳥王子'''とも）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。街道の起点にある[[窪津王子]]から数えて8番目。
その存在は、[[1201年]]（建仁元年）、後鳥羽上皇の熊野御幸の記録『後鳥羽院熊野御幸記』に「大鳥居新王子」として記されており、この頃に比較的近い時期に設けられたものと推定される。「大鳥居」の名称は、付近の[[大鳥神社]]とその鳥居に由来するとの説が有力である。
その位置は、江戸時代まで存在した北王子村にあった大鳥美波比神社（現在は大鳥神社に合祀）といわれている。現在の[[堺市]]鳳東町7のNTT鳳営業所付近と考えられる。
*最寄駅 [[JR]][[阪和線]][[鳳駅]]

'''大鳥居王子'''（おおとりいおうじ、'''大鳥王子'''とも）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。街道の起点にある[[窪津王子]]から数えて8番目。
その存在は、[[1201年]]（建仁元年）、後鳥羽上皇の熊野御幸の記録『後鳥羽院熊野御幸記』に「大鳥居新王子」として記されており、この頃に比較的近い時期に設けられたものと推定される。「大鳥居」の名称は、付近の[[大鳥大社|大鳥神社]]とその鳥居に由来するとの説が有力である。
その位置は、江戸時代まで存在した北王子村にあった大鳥美波比神社（現在は大鳥神社に合祀）といわれている。現在の[[堺市]]鳳東町7のNTT鳳営業所付近と考えられる。

'''大鳥居王子'''（おおとりいおうじ、'''大鳥王子'''とも）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。街道の起点にある[[窪津王子]]から数えて8番目。
その存在は、[[1201年]]（建仁元年）、後鳥羽上皇の熊野御幸の記録『後鳥羽院熊野御幸記』に「大鳥居新王子」として記されており、この頃に比較的近い時期に設けられたものと推定される。「大鳥居」の名称は、付近の[[大鳥大社|大鳥神社]]とその鳥居に由来するとの説が有力である。
その位置は、[[大鳥郡]]北王子村にあった大鳥美波比神社といわれており、現在の[[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]鳳東町7丁の[[西日本電信電話|NTT]]鳳営業所付近と考えられる。なお、大鳥美波比神社は[[1879年]]（明治12年）に大鳥神社境内へ遷座されている。
* 最寄駅 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[阪和線]][[鳳駅]]

revision.comment: Adding an intro to all of them. It has a magic word instead of a substitution because all of them are gonna be moved in the future to proper pagenames. See comment for more info.
revision.text:
''{{PAGENAME}}'' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview==

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.text:
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] to [[Category:new pages]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:new pages]] tagging all the 99 Oji as new original pages using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

[[ja:大鳥居王子]][[zh:大鳥居王子]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[en:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Kujūku Ōji|bottom}}[[Category:sqq]]
[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] ]] [[ja:大鳥居王子]][[zh:大鳥居王子]]

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] ]] [[ja:大鳥居王子]][[zh:大鳥居王子]]

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] ]] [[ja:大鳥居王子]][[zh:大鳥居王子]]

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | Image = | Location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | Coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== Location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji}][[Category:qq]] ]] [[ja:大鳥居王子]][[zh:大鳥居王子]]

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | Deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:new pages]] ]] [[ja:大鳥居王子]][[zh:大鳥居王子]]

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * **{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}** on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] ]] [[ja:大鳥居王子]][[zh:大鳥居王子]]

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}
