revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] to [[Category:Completion tier 1]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qqq]] ]] [[ja:大鳥居王子]][[zh:大鳥居王子]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} [[ja:Ōtorii Ōji]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

[[Category:Kujūku Ōji template forks]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:goodbye $1]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]] ]] [[ja:大鳥居王子]][[zh:大鳥居王子]] {{Kujūku Ōji}} [[ja:Ōtorii Ōji]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtorii Ōji]][[Category:qq]]

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} - '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|九十九王子" to "|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji"
revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}
