revision.comment: Text replacement - "九十九王子|" to "九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|"
revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}
{{Shinto Sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūkuōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. <!--subst:PAGENAME magic word should be substituted in the future when this is moved to its final pagename. This was added to a few pages not created on Feb 8 because they were very barren and this gives context to the fact that they are in a large group of pages all to be translated more or less as a group similar to the Ise Shrines--> ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}, {{ill|Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}} (around 1201) | Map = }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin era|ja|建仁}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市西区}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|ja|鳳駅}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|ja|阪和線}} operated by {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kōdō|ja|熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記| date = 2024年5月}} '''大鳥居王子'''（おおとりいおうじ、'''大鳥王子'''とも）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。街道の起点にある[[窪津王子]]から数えて8番目。
その存在は、[[1201年]]（建仁元年）、[[後鳥羽天皇|後鳥羽上皇]]の熊野御幸の記録『後鳥羽院熊野御幸記』に「大鳥居新王子」として記されており、この頃に比較的近い時期に設けられたものと推定される。「大鳥居」の名称は、付近の[[大鳥大社|大鳥神社]]とその鳥居に由来するとの説が有力である。
その位置は、[[大鳥郡]]北王子村にあった大鳥美波比神社といわれており、現在の[[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]鳳東町7丁の[[西日本電信電話|NTT]]鳳営業所付近と考えられる。なお、大鳥美波比神社は[[1879年]]（明治12年）に大鳥神社境内へ遷座されている。
* 最寄駅 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[阪和線]][[鳳駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] *: [[境王子]] - '''大鳥居王子''' - [[篠田王子]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとりいおうし}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]] [[Category:九十九王子]] [[Category:堺市西区の建築物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[大鳥居王子]] to [[Ōtorii Ōji]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{出典の明記| date = 2024年5月}} '''大鳥居王子'''（おおとりいおうじ、'''大鳥王子'''とも）は、[[熊野街道]]沿いに設けられた[[九十九王子]]の一つ。街道の起点にある[[窪津王子]]から数えて8番目。
その存在は、[[1201年]]（建仁元年）、[[後鳥羽天皇|後鳥羽上皇]]の熊野御幸の記録『後鳥羽院熊野御幸記』に「大鳥居新王子」として記されており、この頃に比較的近い時期に設けられたものと推定される。「大鳥居」の名称は、付近の[[大鳥大社|大鳥神社]]とその鳥居に由来するとの説が有力である。
その位置は、[[大鳥郡]]北王子村にあった大鳥美波比神社といわれており、現在の[[堺市]][[西区 (堺市)|西区]]鳳東町7丁の[[西日本電信電話|NTT]]鳳営業所付近と考えられる。なお、大鳥美波比神社は[[1879年]]（明治12年）に大鳥神社境内へ遷座されている。
* 最寄駅 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[阪和線]][[鳳駅]]
== 関連項目 == * [[熊野信仰]] * [[熊野古道]] - [[紀伊路]] * [[九十九王子]] *: [[境王子]] - '''大鳥居王子''' - [[篠田王子]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとりいおうし}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市西区の歴史]] [[Category:九十九王子]] [[Category:堺市西区の建築物]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|qq=|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|qid=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|qq=|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|qq=|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|qq=|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|qq=|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|qq=|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|qq=|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Ōji|qq=|1=Shinoda Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603815}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|qq=|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|qq=|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|qq=|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|qq=|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|qq=|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Ōji|1=Shinoda Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603815}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Ōji|1=Shinoda Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603815}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Ōji|1=Shinoda Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603815}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Ōji|1=Shinoda Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603815}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大鳥居王子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11441988]] Wikidata name: Ōtorii-ōji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ([[:en:Kennin|Kennin era]] 1) record of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Go-Toba]]'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Sakai|Nishi-ku, Sakai]].
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''[[:en:Ōtori Station|Ōtori Station]]''' on the [[:en:Hanwa Line|Hanwa Line]] operated by [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]].
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Ōji|1=Shinoda Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603815}}
