'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai, Osaka|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin|en|Kennin|WD=Q1195433|lt=Kennin era|1=Kennin}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|en|Ōtori Station|WD=Q857394|lt=Ōtori Station|1=Ōtori Station}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}} operated by {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}.

'''Ōtorii Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]].
==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}} | image = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai, Osaka|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = | deity = | Affiliation = {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kumano Pilgrimage|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Pilgrimage|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} | Established = {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} (around 1201) | Map = | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1042499|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}} }}
'''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' (おおとりいおうじ), also known as '''{{ill|Ōtori Ōji|ja|大鳥王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', was the 8th of the {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines along the historic {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} pilgrimage route, counting from the starting point at {{ill|Kubotsu Ōji|ja|窪津王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubotsu Ōji|1=Kubotsu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597673}}.
== History == The existence of Ōtorii Ōji is documented in the 1201 ({{ill|Kennin|en|Kennin|WD=Q1195433|lt=Kennin era|1=Kennin}} 1) record of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano, titled *{{ill|Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja|後鳥羽院熊野御幸記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Go-Toba-in Kumano Gyokō-ki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, where it is referred to as "Ōtorii Shin Ōji" (大鳥居新王子). This suggests that the shrine was established relatively close to that period.
The name "Ōtorii" (meaning "Great Torii Gate") is believed to originate from its association with the nearby {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha|1=Ōtori taisha}} and its prominent torii gate.
== location == Ōtorii Ōji was located in the historical {{ill|Ōtori District, Izumi|ja|大鳥郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori District, Izumi|1=Ōtori district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270651}}'s Kita-Ōji Village, believed to correspond to the current area around the {{ill|NTT Ōtori Office|ja|NTT鳳営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NTT Ōtori Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōtori Higashi-chō|ja|鳳東町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Higashi-chō|1=Otori-higashi-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92057672}} 7-chōme, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Sakai|en|Nishi-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042499|lt=Nishi-ku, Sakai|1=Nishi-ku, Sakai}}.
It is also associated with the {{ill|Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja|大鳥美波比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Minabehi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was relocated in 1879 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 12) to the grounds of Ōtori Shrine.
== Access == * '''{{ill|Ōtori Station|en|Ōtori Station|WD=Q857394|lt=Ōtori Station|1=Ōtori Station}}''' on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|WD=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}} operated by {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}.
== See also == * {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|WD=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|1=Kiiji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} *: {{ill|Sakai Ōji|ja|境王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Ōji|1=Sakai Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429062}} * '''{{ill|Ōtorii Ōji|ja|大鳥居王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtorii Ōji|1=Ōtorii Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441988}}''' - {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Ōji|1=Shinoda Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603815}}
